summary: Added a replay-resistant exact-byte materialization execution boundary that remains inert unless an authorized plan, unchanged repository head, matching byte digest, and injected protected writer are all present.
observed: The plan binds exact canonical bytes and their SHA-256 digest to one authority request.
Committed request digests in the supplied replay ledger are rejected.
Execution refuses a moved repository head or altered canonical bytes.
The module cannot write by itself; mutation requires an injected protected writer.
Five deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical pre-commit source.
inferred: The continuity pipeline now separates snapshot construction, reconciliation, authority, execution planning, mutation injection, and post-mutation receipt generation.
proposed: Wire the executor to a protected GitHub workflow that authenticates actor and workflow provenance and persists receipts in an append-only replay ledger.
superseded: An authorized decision alone is sufficient to replace canonical queue state.
A previously committed authorization request may be safely reused.
unresolved: The committed tests have not been observed in repository CI.
No protected writer or cryptographically authenticated workflow identity is connected.
No canonical queue mutation was performed in this run.
privacy_boundary: Public repository artifacts and synthetic test fixtures only; no raw private chat, health data, credentials, identifying personal information, or unreviewed uploads.
falsification_route: Run the committed tests in a clean checkout, then attempt replay, head movement, digest alteration, path substitution, denied authority, and a writer response without a full commit SHA. Any accepted unsafe case falsifies the boundary.
